title: Date and walnut cake
subtitle: A sticky British teatime cake with medjool dates and toasted walnuts
excerpt: Date and walnut cake is a British teatime staple: dense, sticky and not too sweet. Soaking the dates in boiling water and bicarbonate of soda softens them and gives the crumb its dark colour and toffee-like flavour. The walnuts add a slightly bitter crunch.
sourceNotes: Date and walnut cake is a British teatime cake with roots in post-war rationing-era baking, when dates provided sweetness when sugar was scarce. The soaking technique using bicarbonate of soda to soften the dates appears in mid-twentieth-century British cookbooks and is consistent with the established formula for sticky date pudding. This recipe follows the established British date cake tradition.

recipe.freezeNotes: Slice and freeze for up to 3 months. Thaw at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The cake keeps for 5 days at room temperature, well wrapped. It improves over the first day as the dates soften further.

glossaryTerms.definition: Soaking chopped dates in boiling water with bicarbonate of soda has two effects. The hot water softens the fruit so it breaks down into a paste during mixing. The bicarbonate of soda, which is alkaline, reacts with the natural acids in the dates and darkens them, producing the characteristic deep brown colour and slightly toffee-like flavour that defines date cake.

content.text: is what turns this from a plain fruit cake into something with depth. Do not skip it. Leave the dates to soak for at least 10 minutes until they have absorbed most of the water and can be crushed to a rough paste with a fork.

Put the 200 g chopped dates in a bowl. Pour over 200 ml boiling water and stir in the 1 tsp bicarbonate of soda. Leave for 10 to 15 minutes until the dates have softened and absorbed most of the water. Mash roughly with a fork to a coarse paste.

Heat the oven to 170 degrees C. Grease a 900 g loaf tin and line with baking paper. Beat the 100 g softened butter and 100 g dark brown sugar until light. Beat in the 2 eggs and 1 tsp vanilla extract. Stir in the date paste (including any remaining liquid), then fold in the 200 g self-raising flour and quarter teaspoon salt. Stir in the 80 g toasted walnuts. Scrape into the tin and level the top. Bake for 45 to 55 minutes until a skewer comes out with a few moist crumbs and the top is deep brown. Cool in the tin for 15 minutes before turning out.

items.problem: Cake is very dark on top but underbaked inside
items.solution: Cover loosely with a piece of foil after 30 minutes to protect the top. The dark sugar and date paste make this cake colour faster than a pale sponge. Continue baking and test with a skewer.

items.problem: Texture is very dense and gummy
items.solution: The dates were not properly soaked and mashed, or the loaf was underbaked. The soaked dates should break down to a spreadable paste. If the loaf is still dense after cooling, it needed another 5 to 10 minutes in the oven.
